mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter-long horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE meets the trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is rising while housing starts are declining, it indicates a tightening monetary policy successfully cooling an overheating economy, which typically precedes a period of market uncertainty and negative S&P 500 returns as growth slows.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% or more in a month AND HOUST declines by 5% or more month-over-month
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and HOUST meet their respective trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in industrial production coupled with rising consumer prices suggests demand outstripping supply, leading to inflationary pressures that erode corporate margins and investor confidence, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth > 5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth > 4%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 45% of instances where both industrial production and CPIAUCSL meet their trigger conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in GDP growth indicates a weakening economic expansion, which reduces future earnings expectations for companies and thus exerts downward pressure on S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following a GDPC1 growth rate falling below the 1.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 55% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is at a historically low level, it signals an accommodative monetary policy designed to stimulate economic activity, which typically boosts asset prices and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of persistently high inflation and stagnant industrial production indicates stagflationary pressures, where rising costs are not offset by increased output, leading to a significant drag on corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and INDPRO meet their respective trigger conditions.
